description: Will Stanton travels to Wales to recover from a serious illness and finds himself drawn into the next stage of his quest as an Old One, the ancient guardians of the Light. Hidden in the Welsh mountains is a magical golden harp that has the power to wake six ancient sleepers who will aid the Light in the final battle against the Dark. But the Grey King, a powerful lord of the Dark, guards the harp and commands terrifying fog and shadow creatures that haunt the mountainside. This Newbery Medal-winning fourth book in the Dark Is Rising sequence is a beautifully written and suspenseful adventure steeped in Welsh mythology.

series_author_group: Susan Cooper — The Dark Is Rising
title: Silver on the Tree
author: Susan Cooper
description: The Dark is making its final and most powerful bid to conquer the world, and Will Stanton, the Drew children, and the mysterious Bran Davies must unite for the last great battle between Light and Dark. Their quest leads them through time itself to find the crystal sword that can cut the final link between the Dark and its power in the mortal world. Each member of the group faces personal tests and sacrifices as the forces of the Dark grow ever stronger around them. This magnificent conclusion to the Dark Is Rising sequence brings the five-book saga to a deeply moving and unforgettable end.

title: Minecraft: The Island
author: Max Brooks
description: In this official Minecraft novel by World War Z author Max Brooks, a mysterious hero wakes up stranded on a remote island in the blocky world of Minecraft with no memory and no idea how they got there. With nothing but their bare hands and their wits, they must learn to survive in this harsh environment, mining for resources, crafting tools, and building shelter against the hostile creatures that emerge at night. As the hero explores the island's secrets, they uncover clues about their past and the deeper mysteries of this strange world. This thrilling survival story captures the spirit of Minecraft while telling a compelling tale about resilience, creativity, and never giving up.

title: Minecraft: The Crash
author: Tracey Baptiste
description: After a terrible car accident, sixteen-year-old Bianca Cooper escapes into the familiar world of Minecraft to cope with her trauma and guilt. But what starts as a comforting retreat quickly turns into a nightmare when Bianca realizes she is trapped inside the game with no way out. The hostile in-game characters that attack her bear an unsettling resemblance to people from her real life, forcing her to confront the painful memories she has been running from. This gripping Minecraft novel explores themes of grief, friendship, and healing through the lens of the beloved video game world.

title: Antboy
author: Kenneth B. Andersen
description: Twelve-year-old Pelle is a short, chubby kid at the bottom of the schoolyard pecking order who dreams of being something more. Then one day he is bitten by a radioactive ant and develops incredible superpowers, including super strength and the ability to produce formic acid. Taking on the secret identity of Antboy, he fights injustice and crime in his quiet Danish town. But being a superhero is not as glamorous as it seems in the movies, and when a real villain named the Flea emerges, Pelle discovers that saving the day requires more than just powers. This charming and funny superhero story proves that even the most unlikely kid can become a hero.

title: Klawde: Evil Alien Warlord Cat
author: Johnny Marciano
description: Klawde is not your ordinary house cat. He is actually Wyss-Kull, a feared and ruthless warlord from the planet Lyttyrboks, who has been overthrown and exiled to the worst planet in the universe: Earth. Forced to live with twelve-year-old Raj Banerjee, who has just moved to a new town and has problems of his own, Klawde plots his return to power while grudgingly accepting belly rubs and tuna treats. As Klawde schemes to reclaim his throne across the galaxy, he and Raj form an unlikely and hilarious friendship. This laugh-out-loud sci-fi comedy tells both sides of the story, alternating between the perspectives of a boy and his diabolical alien cat.
